title: My MGEM Capstone Project
text:
My MGEM capstone projects takes a close look at the Cable Bay area in Nanaimo, BC to investigate spatial and temporal changes in shoreline habitats around Cable Bay using remote sensing and geomatics techniques. The project aims to provide a detailed understanding of how shoreline land cover types have shifted over the period 2020 to 2025, supporting conservation and environmental management efforts in the region.
Using high-resolution satellite imagery and geospatial analysis tools, the study identifies patterns in shoreline vegetation, intertidal zones, and other ecological features. The project leverages land cover classification, image differencing, and GIS-based spatial analysis to quantify habitat changes and map areas of significant ecological transformation.
This work highlights my interest in the intersection of ecology and geomatics, demonstrating how remote sensing can be applied to monitor environmental change, support data-driven decision-making, and inform environmental management practices. The findings of this project aim to provide valuable insights for the city of Nanaimo and conservation initiatives focused on the environmental preservation of the area.

title: My MGEM Capstone
My MGEM Capstone
About Me!
text:
How did I get here
My name is Grace Lush, a graduate of the University of Guelph and a current Master’s student at the University of British Columbia. I completed my undergraduate degree in Environmental Science in 2025, where I developed a strong foundation in ecological systems, environmental analysis, and applied environmental management.
During my undergraduate studies, I completed several co-op positions in environmental-related roles, which provided valuable hands-on experience and helped bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world environmental work. These positions allowed me to gain practical skills while exploring different areas of the environmental field.
I am currently pursuing a Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management at UBC. Through this program, I have developed a strong interest in geomatics and remote sensing, particularly in how spatial data and satellite imagery can be used to analyze and monitor environmental change.
My academic and professional interests lie at the intersection of ecology and geospatial technology. I am particularly interested in how remote sensing and spatial analysis can be used to better understand ecological patterns, monitor landscapes, and support environmental decision-making.
Outside of Academics
When I’m not exploring the environment through my studies, you can usually find me outdoors enjoying nature—whether hiking, swimming, or simply soaking in a scenic view. I have a deep love for animals and find immense joy from any wildlife encounter or spending time with my pets.
Traveling is one of my favorite ways to experience new landscapes, and I love seeking out outdoor adventures wherever I go. I also enjoy staying active through sports, from casual games with friends to exploring new activities. These experiences outside of academics fuel my curiosity, creativity, and appreciation for the world I study in my research.

title: Resume
text:
Remote Sensing & GIS: - ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, and spatial data analysis
- Imaging spectroscopy and hyperspectral analysis
- LiDAR data processing and 3D terrain modeling
- Satellite imagery interpretation - Land cover classification, change detection, and mapping
Environmental & Laboratory Skills: - PCR, DNA extraction, differential media testing
- Soil and water sampling, site assessment, and field surveys
- Environmental monitoring and technical reporting
Data & Programming: - R and Python for environmental data analysis and visualization
- Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
- Database management and data cleaning

title: Resume
text:
Soft Skills

Communication: Clearly convey technical and complex information in both professional and client-facing settings; adept at explaining procedures, findings, and recommendations

Teamwork & Collaboration: Work effectively with research teams, lab colleagues, and service staff to achieve goals and ensure smooth operations

Customer Service & Client Relations: Built strong relationships with clients and customers, resolving concerns professionally and ensuring positive experiences

Organization & Time Management: Successfully manage multiple tasks, projects, and responsibilities in fieldwork, laboratory settings, and fast-paced service environments

Problem Solving & Adaptability: Quickly identify solutions in dynamic situations, whether in research projects, environmental fieldwork, or high-pressure customer service

Leadership & Mentoring: Guided junior team members, trained new staff, and supported colleagues in both academic and professional settings
